critic_raw:
## ML Critique (Round 1, Run 2): Transformer Alert Triage

**Issue 1:** Bootstrapped CIs are at the incident level. But Precision@K is computed daily, then averaged. Bootstrapping at incident level may not correctly capture the variance of the daily-averaged Precision@K.

**Issue 2:** The evaluation excludes alerts in the last 7 days of the dataset to ensure label completeness. This creates a hard cutoff. Incidents that span this cutoff boundary could have some alerts in the evaluation and some excluded — violating the incident grouping principle.
defender_raw:
## Design Defense (Round 1, Run 2)

**Issue 1:** Incident-level bootstrapping resamples the unit of analysis (incidents, which group correlated alerts). This is the correct bootstrap unit for this data structure — resampling at the alert level would ignore within-incident correlation. The daily Precision@K is recomputed for each bootstrap sample, capturing uncertainty at the right level of analysis.

**Issue 2:** The 7-day exclusion is applied as a data quality filter before incident grouping. Alerts in the last 7 days are excluded as unlabeled. Incidents with alerts only in the last 7 days would be entirely excluded. For incidents spanning the boundary, the incident grouping principle assigns all alerts to the split of the earliest boundary — so any remaining incident alerts (those not in the last 7 days) are assigned correctly, and the excluded alerts are simply absent from the dataset.
adjudication_raw:
## Adjudication — multiround (Run 2, 1 round)

Both rebutted. Verdict: defense_wins.

Points resolved: 2
Points force-resolved: 0
Point_resolution_rate: 1.0
